1.14: Firms may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the seller.
1.15: Firms may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Name and address of seller's solicitor or licensed conveyancer required.
Details must include both the firm and the specific individual acting.
3.1.5: No other documents are acceptable for identity verification.
3.2.3: Name and address of seller's solicitor or licensed conveyancer required.
Details must include both the firm and the specific individual acting.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted subject to firm procedures and indemnity cover.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to firm procedures and indemnity cover.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents are not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development/established use certificates are not required.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if they comprise only part of the title.
5.8.1: Converted buildings with up to four flats are acceptable if the borrower occupies one, owns the freehold (or another flat owner does), and other flats are on long leases.
5.8.5: Acceptable for security to comprise one of two leasehold flats where owners hold the cross-reversionary freehold of each other's units.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee or insolvent landlords is accepted.
Indemnity limit must equal the purchase price or valuation.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term is the mortgage term plus 30 years.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted.
Indemnity limit must equal the purchase price or valuation.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts are not required.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is not required.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates must be sent after completion with the title deeds.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms must be sent after completion with the title deeds.
5.15.2d: Management company's memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.17.5a: Powers of attorney must be sent after completion with the title deeds.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation of power of attorney must be sent after completion with the title deeds.
6.4.4: CML Disclosure of Incentives Form required for first-time occupations or redevelopments.
Form must be provided prior to exchange or completion.
Discrepancies from the application may result in re-offer or withdrawal.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copy of tenancy agreement is not required.
6.6.4: Houses in multiple occupation (HMO) are accepted.
Refer cases to underwriting if the offer does not explicitly acknowledge HMO status.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation must be placed with title deeds.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be placed with title deeds.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate is not required.
6.10.2: Retentions or advance instalments are released to the borrower.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required from all occupants aged 17 or over.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise any specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent after completion with the title deeds.
9.2: Indemnity limit for purchases: the higher of purchase price or valuation.
Indemnity limit for remortgages: 115% of the loan amount.
10.2a: Advances are paid electronically unless a cheque is specifically requested by the borrower.
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance is 3 working days prior to completion.
10.3: Standard deductions include £35.00 funds release fee, arrangement fees, higher lending charges, or retentions where applicable.
10.7: Unused advances must be returned after 48 hours via same-day electronic transfer without deduction.
10.9: Mortgage Processing Department must be notified by telephone immediately if completion is delayed.
10.10: Advances may be held for a maximum of 48 hours.
10.11: Daily interest is charged on delayed returns at the rate specified in the Offer of Advance.
14.1.4: Form CH2 required if the existing mortgage deed does not permit further advances.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deed and other original title documents must be sent.
14.2.2: For residential owner-occupied properties, only the Title Information Document and Mortgage Deed are retained; all others are sent to the borrower.
For commercial and buy-to-let mortgages, all documents are sent to the Society.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation involves the Society seal, an authorised signatory, and authority from the Board of Directors.
16.3.1: No standard form of transfer or deed of covenant exists.
16.3.4: Transfer of equity must be placed with title deeds.
16.4.2: Copy of proposed tenancy is not required.
17.2.1b: Discharge is processed via Form DS1.
